items.headline: Referees' chief says disallowing Hammers goal against Arsenal 'categorically' right
items.description: Referees' chief Howard Webb has said disallowing what would have been a key West Ham equaliser during Sunday's 1-0 defeat by Arsenal was a "categorically" correct decision...

items.headline: MLB players, owners start collective bargaining, 6 1/2 months ahead of contract's expiration
items.description:
NEW YORK (AP) — Negotiators for baseball players and owners began what figures to be lengthy and acrimonious collective bargaining negotiations Tuesday to replace their labor contract that expires Dec. 1, with management likely to propose a salary cap system the union has vowed never to accept.
An initial session of about two hours took place at the office of the Major League Baseball Players Association, a five-minute walk from Major League Baseball's headquarters in Manhattan's Rockefeller Center. The meeting lasted about two hours and was scheduled for initial presentations from each side on their view of the sport and its economics. No proposals were made.

items.headline: The Arizona Diamondbacks' rotation makes an abrupt turnaround following ugly stretch
items.description:
PHOENIX (AP) — The walks were piling up, homers flying out of the ballpark, runs racing across the plate.
Arizona Diamondbacks manager Torey Lovullo had seen enough.

items.headline: UCLA, South Carolina, UConn and St. John's to play in women's basketball doubleheader in November
items.description:
Geno Auriemma and Dawn Staley's teams will meet for the first time since the two coaches had a tense exchange at the end of their Final Four matchup.
UConn and South Carolina will play at the annual Basketball Hall of Fame Women's Showcase at Mohegan Sun in Connecticut on Nov. 24.

items.headline: Memphis Grizzlies forward Brandon Clarke has died at the age of 29, the team, the NBA and his agency say
items.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Memphis Grizzlies forward Brandon Clarke has died at the age of 29, the team, the NBA and his agency say.

items.headline: Memphis Grizzlies forward Brandon Clarke dies at 29
items.description:
MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Memphis Grizzlies forward Brandon Clarke has died, the team, his agency and the NBA said Tuesday. He was 29.
Neither the Grizzlies nor Clarke's agency, Priority Sports, immediately provided any details about when, where or how Clarke died.

items.headline: Rory McIlroy hits Hollywood, dines at the White House, still finds time to win grand slam
items.description:
NEWTOWN SQUARE, Pa. (AP) — Rory McIlroy can be spotted on screen in movies featuring Hollywood’s biggest stars.
He had speaking lines — pulling off a “Saved by the Bell” joke — in Adam Sandler's “Happy Gilmore 2” and even snagged a bit part for his wife alongside him for a party scene in the recent Anne Hathaway movie “The Devil Wears Prada 2.”

items.headline: Rhythm king Rabada skittles Hyderabad to blast Gujarat to top of IPL
items.description: South Africa's Kagiso Rabada led an inspired pace attack to power Gujarat Titans to the top of the IPL table with an 82-run thrashing...

items.headline: Rome derby row as authorities reschedule Serie A to avoid tennis clash
items.description: Serie A are to appeal the rescheduling of this weekend's Rome derby which city authorities moved from Sunday to Monday to avoid clashing with...

items.headline: Andy Murray to join Jack Draper’s coaching team for Wimbledon
items.description:
LONDON (AP) — Andy Murray will join Jack Draper’s coaching team for the upcoming grass-court season, including Wimbledon.
Draper, a U.S. Open semifinalist in 2024, was ranked as high as No. 4 last year but he has struggled with injuries and is now down to No. 50.
